home.intro: Every mapped place below is sourced live from OpenStreetMap. Click any point to see details, photos and descriptions.
home.howSub: Three steps from idle scrolling to your next trip.
home.step1Body: Pan and zoom the live map, or search by name and country to find {noun} anywhere in the world.
home.step2Body: Click any point for photos, descriptions, opening hours and what's nearby — all in one place.
home.step3Body: Found somewhere you love? Jump straight to tours, tickets and experiences for it.
home.t1Quote: I planned an entire weekend around three places I'd never have found otherwise. The map is ridiculously easy to lose an hour in.
home.t2Quote: Finally a map that's actually complete. I checked my home region and even the obscure spots were on it.
home.t3Quote: The photos and the 'nearby' list make trip planning so quick. It's now my first stop before any road trip.

about.heading: About
about.contributeBody: This map is only as complete as OpenStreetMap itself. If you know a {noun} that isn't here, you can add it directly to OpenStreetMap — it's free, open and will appear here at the next refresh.
about.contributeCta: Add a missing place on OpenStreetMap →
about.reportHeading: Report an error or a wrong location
about.reportBody: Found a {noun} in the wrong spot, with outdated details, or listed twice? The data lives in OpenStreetMap, so the quickest fix is a short note there — a local mapper reviews it and the correction flows back to this map at the next refresh.
about.contactBody: Questions, feedback, or a partnership idea? We'd love to hear from you — reach us at
about.faqBody: Quick answers to the most common questions are on our

faq.heading: Frequently asked questions
faq.intro: Common questions about this map and the {noun} on it.
faq.a1: {name} is a free, interactive world map of every {noun}, built entirely from open OpenStreetMap data. Click any point for a photo, a description and the key facts.
faq.q2: Where does the data come from?
faq.a2: Every location is sourced from OpenStreetMap, the free, community-maintained map of the world. Photos and descriptions come from Wikipedia, Wikimedia Commons and Mapillary.
faq.q3: How often is the map updated?
faq.a3: The dataset is refreshed automatically from OpenStreetMap about once a month, so newly added or edited {noun} show up without any manual work.
faq.a4: Yes. The map is completely free to browse, and the underlying data is open under the Open Database License (ODbL).
faq.q5: How do I find a specific place?
faq.a5: Open the map and use the search box to filter by name or country, or browse by country from the home page.
faq.q6: A {noun} is missing — can I add it?
faq.a6: Absolutely, and we'd love you to. Add it directly to OpenStreetMap — it's free and open — and it will appear here at the next monthly refresh.
faq.q7: Something is wrong or in the wrong place. How do I report it?
faq.a7: Because the data lives in OpenStreetMap, the quickest fix is a short note there. A local mapper reviews it and the correction flows back to this map automatically.
faq.q8: Can I use the data for my own project?
faq.a8: Yes, under the OpenStreetMap ODbL license — just credit "OpenStreetMap contributors". Photos and text keep their original Wikipedia and Wikimedia licenses.
faq.q9: Why don't some places have a photo or description?
faq.a9: We only show a photo or description when one is available from Wikipedia, Wikimedia Commons or Mapillary. Many {noun} simply haven't been documented there yet.
faq.q10: How can I get in touch?
faq.a10: Use the contact link in the footer or on the About page — we're always glad to hear feedback, corrections and ideas.
